title: ISE Custom Posture Provisioning (CPP) Install and Update Audit

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.7 — agent deployment as malware protection.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Break the agent download server. Many install failures. UC-17.1.64 fires.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Posture* category — CPP codes `15080` (CPP provisioning initiated), `15081` (CPP installed), `15082` (CPP install failed), `15083` (CPP update initiated), `15084` (CPP update completed), `15085` (CPP update failed).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (15080, 15081, 15082, 15083, 15084, 15085)
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode IN ("15082","15085"))) as failures by posture_policy
| where failures > 10
description: Tracks Cisco Custom Posture Provisioning (CPP) installation and update flows; detects high failure rates that suggest installer or signing-cert issues.
value: Endpoints can't run posture checks without CPP installed. Install failures break the entire posture chain for affected endpoints.
implementation: Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 4 hours; trigger on count > 0; throttle on `policy_name` for 1 hour.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Initial rollout — high install failures during the first day.
mitreAttack: N/A (policy hygiene — measures Continuous Posture Polling effectiveness, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: Before a computer can be checked for compliance, a small program must be installed on it. We watch how often that installation fails so we know when many users can't get the program.
